title: Derived Categories of the Permutahedral Varieties
speaker: Mario Sanchez
abstract: The derived category of a variety is an important and difficult invariant. In this talk, I discuss a purely convex-geometric and combinatorial approach to these categories for toric varieties. Along the way, we will run into the curious question of studying the contractibility of set differences of polytopes.  I will focus on the toric variety of the permutahedron which has played an important role in many recent developments in matroid theory. I will discuss how this derived category can be described through the theory of matroids.

proof_stages.label: Matroid valuations and the Schubert-matroid indicator basis
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins from strong valuations on polytope families, specializes to matroid polytopes, and recalls that indicator functions of matroid polytopes have a Schubert-matroid basis, so valuative invariants are determined by their Schubert-matroid values.

proof_stages.label: Toric K-theory as a natural target for categorifying valuations
proof_stages.summary: Because many matroid valuations are shadows of richer algebraic or geometric objects, the speaker seeks a categorification and uses toric geometry, where line bundles correspond to polytopes in a deformation cone and K-theory is identified with a space of indicator functions.

proof_stages.label: Permutahedral K-theory basis from generalized permutahedra
proof_stages.summary: For the permutahedral fan, the deformation cone consists of generalized permutahedra, which subdivide into matroid polytopes, yielding a K-theory basis indexed by loopless Schubert matroid polytopes.

proof_stages.label: Derived categories and the coherent-constructible bridge
proof_stages.summary: The derived category of coherent sheaves is presented as the canonical categorification of K-theory, and the coherent-constructible correspondence sends suitable line bundles to constructible sheaves supported on polytopes, categorifying the Morelli indicator-function picture.

proof_stages.label: Convex criteria for full strong exceptional collections
proof_stages.summary: The speaker gives polytope-level criteria—contractibility or acyclicity of set differences for strong exceptionality and weak subdivisions for fullness—and states that these imply the corresponding full strong exceptional collection of line bundles.

proof_stages.label: Combinatorial verification and the main exceptional-collection theorem
proof_stages.summary: After demonstrating the subdivision method in a toric surface case, the speaker states the main theorem: the permutahedral variety and related stellahedral/type-B varieties have full strong exceptional collections indexed by the corresponding Schubert-type matroid polytopes, categorifying known K-theory bases.

proof_stages.label: Quiver models and symmetry consequences
proof_stages.summary: The full strong exceptional collections yield quivers with relations whose vertices and arrows admit matroid-theoretic descriptions, and the construction strengthens previous symmetry-invariant exceptional-collection results for the permutahedral/Losev-Manin setting.

proof_stages.label: Generalizations, failures, and open directions
proof_stages.summary: The final discussion frames the method as applicable to general smooth projective fans, then explores open cases such as associahedra, equivariant variants, type-C obstructions, and possible relationships between different toric realizations.
